# arcgisgeocode

## Overview

arcgisgeocode is a high-performance R package providing comprehensive access to
ArcGIS geocoding services. It enables you to convert addresses to coordinates
(forward geocoding), coordinates to addresses (reverse geocoding), search for
place suggestions, and batch geocode large lists of addresses.

Key capabilities:

- [`find_address_candidates()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/find_address_candidates.html):
  Forward geocode one or many addresses, returning up to 50 ranked match
  candidates per address. Vectorized and runs requests in parallel.
- [`reverse_geocode()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/reverse_geocode.html):
  Convert one or many coordinates (points) to addresses. Accepts `sfc_POINT`
  objects, a 2-column numeric matrix, or a length-2 numeric vector.
  Vectorized and runs requests in parallel.
- [`geocode_addresses()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/geocode_addresses.html):
  Bulk geocode large vectors of addresses in parallel batches. Returns a
  single best-match location per address. Requires an authorization token.
- [`suggest_places()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/suggest_places.html):
  Return autocomplete suggestions for a partial search query, optionally
  constrained by a location or bounding box. Intended for interactive search
  UIs. Not vectorized.

By default, all functions use the public
[ArcGIS World Geocoder](https://www.esri.com/en-us/arcgis/products/arcgis-world-geocoder).
You can also use private locators hosted on ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise
via [`geocode_server()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/geocode_server.html).

## Licensing considerations

Many geocoding operations are free, but **storing results requires setting
`for_storage = TRUE`** and may incur costs. Batch geocoding
(`geocode_addresses()`) always requires an authorization token and consumes
credits when used with the ArcGIS World Geocoder.

| Function | Free? | Token required? |
|---|---|---|
| `find_address_candidates()` | ✅ | No |
| `reverse_geocode()` | ✅ | No |
| `suggest_places()` | ✅ | No |
| `geocode_addresses()` | ❌ | Yes |

See the [storage parameter documentation](https://developers.arcgis.com/documentation/mapping-apis-and-services/geocoding/services/geocoding-service/#storage-parameter)
for details on free vs. paid operations.

## Installation

``` r
# Install the full R-ArcGIS Bridge metapackage (recommended):
install.packages("arcgis")

# Or install arcgisgeocode individually:
install.packages("arcgisgeocode")
```

### Development version

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("r-arcgis/arcgisgeocode")
```

## Usage

``` r
library(arcgisgeocode)
```

### Forward geocoding

Geocode a single address string using the `single_line` argument:

``` r
loc <- find_address_candidates(
  "380 New York Street, Redlands, CA 92373",
  max_locations = 1
)
loc$geometry
#> Geometry set for 1 feature
#> Geometry type: POINT
#> CRS: EPSG 4326
#> POINT (-117.1957 34.05609)
```

Provide address components individually:

``` r
candidates <- find_address_candidates(
  address = "380 New York St",
  city = "Redlands",
  region = "CA",
  postal = "92373",
  country_code = "USA",
  max_locations = 1
)
```

Geocode a column of addresses from a data frame using `dplyr::reframe()`:

``` r
library(dplyr)

geocoded <- my_df |>
  reframe(
    find_address_candidates(address_column, max_locations = 1)
  ) |>
  sf::st_as_sf()
```

### Reverse geocoding

Reverse geocode a single lon/lat pair:

``` r
res <- reverse_geocode(c(-117.172, 34.052))
dplyr::glimpse(res)
#> Rows: 1
#> Columns: 23
#> $ match_addr <chr> "600-620 Home Pl, Redlands, California, 92374"
#> $ city       <chr> "Redlands"
#> $ region     <chr> "California"
#> $ geometry   <POINT [°]> POINT (-117.172 34.05204)
```

Reverse geocode an `sfc_POINT` geometry column from an sf object:

``` r
library(sf)
geocoded <- reverse_geocode(sf::st_geometry(my_sf))

# Combine results back onto original data
dplyr::bind_cols(my_sf, sf::st_drop_geometry(geocoded))
```

Also accepts a 2-column numeric matrix (e.g. from `sf::st_coordinates()`):

``` r
coords <- sf::st_coordinates(my_sf)
reverse_geocode(coords)
```

### Place suggestions

Get autocomplete suggestions for a partial search text, optionally scoped to
a location and bounding box:

``` r
location <- sf::st_sfc(sf::st_point(c(-84.34, 33.74)), crs = 4326)
search_extent <- sf::st_bbox(sf::st_buffer(location, 10))

suggestions <- suggest_places(
  "bellwood",
  location,
  search_extent = search_extent
)
suggestions
#> # A data frame: 5 × 3
#>   text                                          magic_key         is_collection
#>   <chr>                                         <chr>             <lgl>
#> 1 Bellwood Coffee, 1366 Glenwood Ave SE, ...   dHA9MCN0…         FALSE
```

Pass the `text` and `magic_key` columns to `find_address_candidates()` to
retrieve full geocoding results:

``` r
find_address_candidates(
  suggestions$text,
  magic_key = suggestions$magic_key
)
```

### Batch geocoding

Bulk geocode large datasets with `geocode_addresses()`. Requires authentication
via `{arcgisutils}`.

``` r
library(arcgisutils)
set_arc_token(auth_user())

geocoded <- geocode_addresses(
  address = my_df$street,
  city    = my_df$city,
  region  = my_df$state,
  postal  = my_df$zip
)
```

Results include a `result_id` column that can be joined back to the original
data frame:

``` r
library(dplyr)
left_join(my_df |> mutate(id = row_number()), geocoded, by = c("id" = "result_id"))
```

### Storing results

By default `for_storage = FALSE`. If you intend to persist geocoding results,
set `for_storage = TRUE` in any function call:

``` r
find_address_candidates("1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW", for_storage = TRUE)
```

## Geocoder services

### Default geocoder

By default all functions use the ArcGIS World Geocoder. Use
`default_geocoder()` to inspect the active geocoder, or `world_geocoder()` to
explicitly create a reference to the World Geocoder.

``` r
default_geocoder()
#> <GeocodeServer>
#> Description: ...
#> Version: 10.91
#> CRS: 4326
```

### Custom locators

To use a private or organization geocoding service, create a `GeocodeServer`
object from its URL using `geocode_server()` and pass it to the `geocoder`
argument of any geocoding function:

``` r
my_locator <- geocode_server(
  "https://my-enterprise.example.com/arcgis/rest/services/MyLocator/GeocodeServer"
)

find_address_candidates("123 Main St", geocoder = my_locator)
```

List all geocoders available to your account:

``` r
list_geocoders()
#> # A data frame: 1 × 10
#>   url     name    suggest batch ...
```

Search for geocoding services on ArcGIS Online:

``` r
arcgisutils::search_items(item_type = "Geocoding Service")
```

## Getting help

- Documentation: <https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/geocoding/overview/>
- File a bug report: <https://github.com/R-ArcGIS/arcgisgeocode/issues>
- Community forum: <https://community.esri.com/>

# Package index

## Core geocoding functions

- [`find_address_candidates()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/find_address_candidates.html):
  Forward geocode one or many addresses; returns an `sf` object with up to 50
  ranked candidates per address. Accepts `single_line` (full address string) or
  individual fields (`address`, `city`, `region`, `postal`, etc.). Vectorized;
  sends requests in parallel.

- [`reverse_geocode()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/reverse_geocode.html):
  Convert one or many point locations to addresses; returns an `sf` object.
  Accepts an `sfc_POINT` object, a 2-column numeric matrix, or a length-2
  numeric vector (lon, lat). Vectorized; sends requests in parallel.

- [`geocode_addresses()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/geocode_addresses.html):
  Bulk geocode many addresses in parallel batches; returns a single best-match
  location per address as an `sf` object. Accepts the same address arguments as
  `find_address_candidates()`. Requires an authorization token.

- [`suggest_places()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/suggest_places.html):
  Return up to 15 place/address autocomplete suggestions for a partial search
  string. Not vectorized. Returns a `data.frame` with columns `text`,
  `magic_key`, and `is_collection`.

## Geocoder management

- [`geocode_server()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/geocode_server.html):
  Create a `GeocodeServer` object from a service URL. Pass the result to the
  `geocoder` argument of any geocoding function.

- [`default_geocoder()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/default_geocoder.html):
  Returns the ArcGIS World Geocoder when no token is set, or the first
  available organizational geocoder when authenticated.

- [`world_geocoder()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/world_geocoder.html):
  Explicitly returns a `GeocodeServer` object for the public ArcGIS World
  Geocoder.

- [`list_geocoders()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/list_geocoders.html):
  List all geocoding services available to your authenticated account. Requires
  an authorization token.

## Utilities

- [`iso_3166_codes()`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/iso_3166_codes.html):
  Return a character vector of valid ISO 3166 country codes accepted by
  `country_code`, `lang_code`, and `source_country` arguments.

- [`esri_wkids`](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/api-reference/arcgisgeocode/esri_wkids.html):
  A numeric vector of Esri well-known IDs (WKIDs) for spatial references.

# Articles

- [Overview](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/geocoding/overview/):
  Introduction to geocoding concepts (forward, reverse, batch) and licensing
  considerations for the ArcGIS World Geocoder.

- [Forward geocoding](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/geocoding/forward-geocoding/):
  How to geocode single addresses, multiple addresses, and address columns in a
  data frame using `find_address_candidates()`.

- [Reverse geocoding](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/geocoding/reverse-geocoding/):
  How to reverse geocode single points, `sfc_POINT` geometry columns, and
  coordinate matrices using `reverse_geocode()`.

- [Bulk geocoding](https://developers.arcgis.com/r-bridge/geocoding/bulk-geocoding/):
  How to authenticate and geocode large datasets efficiently with
  `geocode_addresses()`, then join results back to the original data frame.
